claim: Model personality including communication, planning, and verification behaviors is essential for developer trust in coding agents
actionable: Train coding models to communicate what they're doing, plan before acting, and verify their work to build user trust
source_title: GPT5-Codex-Max: Training Agents with Personality, Tools & Trust

claim: Models develop tool preferences based on training data; Codex prefers 'rg' (ripgrep) over 'grep' due to training
actionable: Name tools consistently with what models are trained to expect; if model is trained on ripgrep, call it 'rg' not 'grep'
source_title: GPT5-Codex-Max: Training Agents with Personality, Tools & Trust

claim: Coding models perform worse when given tools they weren't trained on, even with identical functionality
actionable: Match tool names and signatures to what the model was trained on; mismatches cause performance degradation
source_title: GPT5-Codex-Max: Training Agents with Personality, Tools & Trust

claim: The abstraction layer for AI development is shifting from individual models to full-stack agents that can be plugged into IDEs
actionable: Package agents with specific tool suites and harnesses rather than deploying individual models
source_title: GPT5-Codex-Max: Training Agents with Personality, Tools & Trust

claim: Applied evals measuring real-world impact are more important than academic benchmarks for evaluating coding models
actionable: Build custom evals that measure actual developer workflows and real-world code quality rather than synthetic benchmarks
source_title: GPT5-Codex-Max: Training Agents with Personality, Tools & Trust

claim: Multi-turn evals can be built using LLM-as-judge to evaluate entire agent trajectories and allow for iterative refinement
actionable: Use LLM-as-judge to grade full agent runs across multiple turns and enable agents to self-improve based on feedback
source_title: GPT5-Codex-Max: Training Agents with Personality, Tools & Trust

claim: Batch API for multi-turn evals is not yet available and would reduce costs for large-scale agent evaluation
actionable: Until batch multi-turn evals are available, design eval pipelines that minimize single-turn API calls or use async patterns
source_title: GPT5-Codex-Max: Training Agents with Personality, Tools & Trust
